chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 182 of 2021 Date Order with signature of Judge For orders on CMA No.1086/2021. 16.04.2021 Mr. Abdur Rahman, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Urgency application is allowed and the instant SMA is taken up. Through the instant petition, Petitioner, Kamran Rashid Ibrahim has prayed for the grant of Succession Certificate in respect of moveable properties/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Properties attached with the memo of petition, available at page 17, which were left by his deceased wife, namely, Seema Kamran Ibrahim, who allegedly died on 04.01.2021 at Karachi, leaving behind her three legal heirs, details whereof is mentioned in paragraph No.3 of the memo of petition. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA and title documents of the immovable properties are also filed at page 11 to 13 and 27 to 39. Legal heirs No.2&3 have executed Power of Attorney, duly attested by the Notary Public at Karachi in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Azhar Hamid S/o Abdul Hamid and Badaruddin Fatehali Vellani S/o Fatehali Walimuhammad Vellani have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. 2 Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 19.03.2021; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 15.04.2021.

Two witnesses, namely, Azhar Hamid S/o Abdul Hamid and Badaruddin Fatehali Vellani S/o Fatehali Walimuhammad Vellani have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. 2 Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 19.03.2021; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 15.04.2021. Today petitioner and witnesses are present. They affirm the contents of petition and their affidavits, and also verify their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Succession Certificate in respect of movable properties/assets shown in the Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Abrar
